attributes.description: Alerts when `kafka.brokers` drops below 2. A broker loss weakens replication and triggers partition reassignments.
attributes.name: [Kafka OTel] Broker count below threshold

investigation_guide.blob:
## Kafka broker count below threshold

### What fired
`kafka.brokers` metric fell below 2 during the window.

### Why it matters
A reduced broker count shrinks available capacity and can leave partitions under-replicated. If replication factor equals lost brokers, data becomes unavailable.

### Triage
1. Check which broker is missing (host-level monitoring).
2. Correlate with under-replicated partitions.
3. Inspect controller election events.

### Remediation
- Bring the missing broker back or replace it.
- Let the cluster re-replicate affected partitions.

### Tuning
- Adjust the minimum count (2) to match your cluster size (e.g. 3 for HA tiers).
